notes: Canonical JOB title rows.
Primary genre is exported as a canonical IMDb genre row; generated subgenres such as Superhero/Martial Arts/Disaster/Experimental are canonicalized and kept verbatim in extras.
Secondary genres are canonicalized and derived only when keyword links provide topic evidence.
Secondary genres use canonicalized repeated company-specialty evidence as a weak signal.
Mapped to canonical IMDb info_type ids where available.
Mapped to canonical IMDb numeric info_type ids where available.
Mapped to canonical IMDb budget/gross info_type ids used by JOB predicates.
Synthetic-only or provenance fields are retained outside strict IMDb core.
Generated filming/location rows map to IMDb locations.
TV-series title rows keep canonical metadata; unmapped generated network names are synthesized as IMDb company rows with source names preserved in extras.
Episode numbering and crew facts map to canonical IMDb columns/rows; release-date country is inherited from the parent series.
Canonical cast rows and character names.
Generated role richness is retained outside strict JOB.
Roles are mapped to IMDb company_type ids.
IMDb-style notes are deterministic derivations.
Canonical company names.
Strict JOB company_name.country_code is deterministically projected to an IMDb-like company-market distribution; source country is retained in extras and company_country_export_audit.csv.
Company lifecycle and preferences are not strict JOB columns.
Canonical person table with phonetic codes.
Generated person profile remains inspectable outside strict JOB.
Canonical keyword table with phonetic codes.
Keyword semantics remain available for audits.
Converted to IMDb-style release-date strings.
Research metadata retained when --include-extras is enabled.
